runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Python", "build_system": "pip", "package_manager": "pip", "build_commands": ["pip install -r requirements.txt", "pip install ."], "install_commands": [], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.12", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "cli_tool", "base_image": "python:3.12.13-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Official Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm base image to run the dirhunt CLI package and install its Python dependencies.", "pitfalls": ["The chosen base image is not slim; image size may be large.", "Some Python wheels may require system build tools; if compilation is needed, you may need to install build-essential and related dev packages.", "setup.py uses requirements.in for install_requires; ensure requirements.txt reflects pinned dependencies for production.", "entry_points expect dirhunt.management:main; ensure package structure preserves this entry point after installation."], "notes": "This blueprint targets a CLI tool (dirhunt). It installs dependencies from requirements.txt and then installs the package from the local directory. The base image is chosen to support Python 3.12 as declared in the repository's supported version list."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Dockerfile for dirhunt - build from source using Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies (needed for some Python packages)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements first to leverage Docker cache
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# Build and install the package from source
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .

# Ensure Python output is not buffered
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Entrypoint and default command
ENTRYPOINT ["dirhunt"]
CMD ["--help"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Dockerfile for dirhunt - build from source using Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies (needed for some Python packages)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements first to leverage Docker cache
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# Build and install the package from source
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .

# Ensure Python output is not buffered
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Entrypoint and default command
ENTRYPOINT ["dirhunt"]
CMD ["--help"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "# Ignore environment and VCS and test related files
.git
__pycache__
*.pyc
*.pyo
*.egg-info
build
dist
venv
.env
.env.*
*.log
*.tox
.tox
*.pytest_cache
.coverage
.coverage.*
htmlcov
.DS_Store

"}

steps.tool_output_raw:
VerifyBuild status: build_failed
Build error:
Failing step
- Step: [5/7] RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
- Exit code: 1
- Command output indicates failure while building wheels for greenlet during pip install

Exact error message (preserved)
- Building wheels for collected packages: greenlet
- error: /tmp/pip-build-env-nxdgrgt6/overlay/lib/python3.12/site-packages/setuptools/dist.py:765: SetuptoolsDeprecationWarning: License classifiers are deprecated.
- error: ‘_PyCFrame’ {aka ‘struct _PyCFrame’} has no member named ‘use_tracing’
- error: ‘const PyThreadState’ {aka ‘const struct _ts’} has no member named ‘recursion_limit’; did you mean ‘py_recursion_limit’?
- error: ‘const PyThreadState’ {aka ‘const struct _ts’} has no member named ‘recursion_remaining’; did you mean ‘c_recursion_remaining’?
- error: ‘const PyThreadState’ {aka ‘const struct _ts’} has no member named ‘trash_delete_nesting’
- error: ‘_PyCFrame’ {aka ‘struct _PyCFrame’} has no member named ‘use_tracing’
- error: ‘PyThreadState’ {aka ‘struct _ts’} has no member named ‘recursion_remaining’; did you mean ‘c_recursion_remaining’?
- error: ‘PyThreadState’ {aka ‘struct _ts’} has no member named ‘recursion_limit’; did you mean ‘py_recursion_limit’?
- error: ‘PyThreadState’ {aka ‘struct _ts’} has no member named ‘trash_delete_nesting’
- error: PyThreadState has no member named ‘trash_delete_nesting’
- error: command '/usr/bin/g++' failed with exit code 1
- Note: This error originates from a subprocess, and is likely not a problem with pip.

Missing packages or files mentioned
- None explicitly missing; the failure is due to compiling greenlet against Python 3.12 ABI changes.

Version/compatibility information
- Base image: python:3.12.13-bookworm
- Issue arises from building greenlet wheel against Python 3.12 APIs (PyThreadState, _PyCFrame changes)
- Likely cause: greenlet version 2.0.2 is not compatible with Python 3.12; requires an updated greenlet (or a Python version compatible with the wheel).

Actionable note
- Consider pinning/updating greenlet to a Python-3.12-compatible release (or using a Python version with ABI compatibility), or patching the greenlet source.

steps.tool_output_raw:
# Dockerfile for dirhunt - build from source using Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies (needed for some Python packages)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements first to leverage Docker cache
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# Build and install the package from source
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .

# Ensure Python output is not buffered
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Entrypoint and default command
ENTRYPOINT ["dirhunt"]
CMD ["--help"]

artifacts.content:
# Dockerfile for dirhunt - build from source using Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies (needed for some Python packages)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements first to leverage Docker cache
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# Build and install the package from source
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir .

# Ensure Python output is not buffered
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Entrypoint and default command
ENTRYPOINT ["dirhunt"]
CMD ["--help"]
